title: Special Education (SPED) School (Sensory impairment)
text:
Special Education (SPED) School (Sensory impairment) - SupportGoWhere
Special Education (SPED) School (Sensory impairment)
Share link
Special Education (SPED) schools provide education for children and youth with disabilities who are of school-going age.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Fees payable
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Government and community-funded Special Education (SPED) schools cater to children and youths with moderate-to-severe special educational needs and require more intensive and specialised support in their schooling journey.

Different SPED schools cater to students with different special educational needs.

Visit MOE Special Education (SPED) schools to learn about the support for students with special educational needs and their educational pathways.

Who is this for?

Lighthouse School offers:

The customised curriculum for students aged 7 to 18 with Hearing Loss; or

The customised curriculum for students aged 7 to 18 with Visual Impairment; or

The national curriculum for students aged 7 to 12 with Visual Impairment (i.e., same curriculum as mainstream primary schools)

What to expect?

Please visit MOE Special Education (SPED) schools and each SPED school’s website for more information on each school.

How to apply?

Please visit MOE Special Education (SPED) schools for details on the application process for SPED schools.

Information last updated on 10 Feb 2026
